typecheck.observation: All checks passed after fixing 30 ty diagnostics (adding type: ignore comments for known PyTorch stub limitations)

appliedUpdates.description: Extended environment.md ty quirks section: documented # type: ignore[operator], # type: ignore[union-attr], torch.outer workarounds, and getattr pattern for nn.Module custom attributes

Extended architecture.md Optimizer Stack: documented in-place grad mutation convention, Newton-Schulz coefficients, bfloat16 internal computation, 1D parameter SGD fallback, momentum shape, Haar normalization, aspect ratio scaling in compressed space, GWTOptimizer vs NorMuonGWT distinction
Extended architecture.md Liger Kernels: documented LigerAugmentedModel CPU pass-through behavior and GPU integration path; LEMA prefetch serialization note

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: when writing code that accesses custom model attributes (e.g., model.blocks, model.config.n_layers) on nn.Module-typed variables, use getattr(model, 'attr', default) with type: ignore comments for ty compatibility.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: training_loop.py had to be fixed during scrutiny validation because model is typed as nn.Module and doesn't expose .blocks or .config.n_layers. Multiple features will need this pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note to 'Workers should update library' section: when completing a feature, update architecture.md with any new implementation details discovered during work (e.g., coefficient values, algorithm variants, known CPU/GPU behavioral differences).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The liger-lema-integration worker correctly updated architecture.md with Liger and LEMA sections. This is a good pattern that should be formalized.

rejectedObservations.observation: Suggest updating design/llm-tech.md with Newton-Schulz coefficient values
rejectedObservations.reason: design/llm-tech.md is a research reference document read during implementation, not a living codebase document. The coefficients are already documented in code comments and now in architecture.md.

rejectedObservations.observation: Skill file review confirms ml-engineer skill is working correctly with no deviations
rejectedObservations.reason: This is informational confirmation, not an actionable update.
